text: The narrative that Ronaldo is hated in Portugal and his team mates are fed up is mostly a media and reddit driven narrative. Playing him might not be the right thing tactically but he is still a legend there.

text: I really like Ferran. He works hard, presses well and knows how to position himself in deadly spots. It's why he gets so many attempts at goal. Unfortunately he hasn't been a great finisher. It's what separates him from being a top 3 striker in the world to being a super sub.
label: analysis

text: He's topping the charts for big chances missed because he gets into great positions all the time, his positional awareness is so good. But unfortunately his finishing lets him down. If he improves his finishing he'll be up there among the top 5 strikers easily, because he has pace, positional sense and works tirelessly. Missing piece is his finishing and first touch.
label: analysis
notes: Ferran thread; detailed assessment w/ chances-missed stat

text: He is one goal away from having a generational run with Spain. Amazing in press, great off the ball movement, intelligent at reading the play, passionate and hard working personality, but he depends on confidence and good form way too much.
